LAW AND MOTION 302,PLAINTIFF WALTER CRUZ'S DEMURRER. PLAINTIFF'S DEMURRER TO DEFENDANTS SILVER ARROWS REMODEL & RESTORATION INC. AND JAIME MELO'S (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS JAMIE MELO) ANSWER IS OVERRULED AS TO DEFENSES ONE AND TWO (COMPARATIVE FAULT DEFENSES) BECAUSE THEY MAY TURN ON FACTUAL ISSUES THAT CANNOT BE DECIDED ON A DEMURRER. THE PRIMARY FUNCTION OF THE ANSWER IS TO GIVE THE OTHER PARTY NOTICE SO THAT IT MAY PREPARE ITS CASE. (HARRIS V. CITY OF SANTA MONICA (2013) 56 CAL.4TH 203, 240.) A PURPORTED DEFECT IN A PLEADING THAT OTHERWISE GIVES NOTICE TO THE OTHER SIDE CANNOT BE SAID TO AFFECT SUBSTANTIAL RIGHTS OR CAUSE PREJUDICE. (ID.) IF, AFTER A REASONABLE OPPORTUNITY FOR DISCOVERY AND FACTUAL INVESTIGATION, PLAINTIFF CONTENDS THAT DEFENDANTS LACK FACTUAL SUPPORT FOR SOME OR ALL OF THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, PLAINTIFF SHOULD REQUEST THAT DEFENDANTS WITHDRAW THOSE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. IF PLAINTIFF IS DISSATISFIED BY THE RESPONSE, THEN HE MAY BRING A SUMMARY ADJUDICATION MOTION SEEKING TO ELIMINATE THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES LACKING FACTUAL SUPPORT. THE DEMURRERS TO DEFENSES THREE AND FIVE (STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS DEFENSES) ARE SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS CONCEDED. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

Off Calendar May-03-2019 Continued To Jul-03-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF WALTER CRUZ's DEMURRER.

Plaintiff's demurrer to Defendants Silver Arrows Remodel & Restoration Inc. and Jaime Melo's (erroneously sued as Jamie Melo) answer is overruled as to defenses one and two (comparative fault defenses) because they may turn on factual issues that cannot be decided on a demurrer. The primary function of the answer is to give the other party notice so that it may prepare its case. (Harris v. City of Santa Monica (2013) 56 Cal.4th 203, 240.) A purported defect in a pleading that otherwise gives notice to the other side cannot be said to affect substantial rights or cause prejudice. (Id.) If, after a reasonable opportunity for discovery and factual investigation, Plaintiff contends that Defendants lack factual support for some or all of the affirmative defenses, Plaintiff should request that Defendants withdraw those affirmative defenses. If Plaintiff is dissatisfied by the response, then he may bring a summary adjudication motion seeking to eliminate the affirmative defenses lacking factual support. The demurrers to defenses three and five (statute of limitations defenses) are sustained without leave to amend as conceded.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
